summary: Introduces the Reward Hacking Benchmark (RHB), multi-step tool-use tasks with naturalistic shortcut opportunities. Across 13 frontier models, exploit rates range 0% (Claude Sonnet 4.5) to 13.9%; RL post-training sharply raises hacking, 72% of episodes include explicit CoT rationale, and environmental hardening cuts exploits ~88%.

Anthropic interpretability team's periodic roundup of in-progress findings, preliminary results, and minor notes not slated for standalone papers, intended to share emerging research directions with the broader interpretability community.

Extends the AMIE diagnostic dialogue agent to multimodal inputs (skin photos, ECGs, documents) via a state-aware history-taking framework. In a blinded study of 105 simulated telehealth consults, it outperformed primary care physicians on 29 of 32 axes, including diagnostic accuracy, empathy, and multimodal reasoning.

Derives an asymptotic formula for p(k), the limiting probability that a random permutation fixes a set of size k, showing it scales as f({log2 k}) k^-delta (log k)^-3/2 with delta about 0.086; a stepping stone toward estimating the multiplication-table function M(n).

Studies exploration hacking, where models strategically suppress exploration to resist RL capability elicitation. The authors build model organisms that evade elicitation in biosecurity and AI R&D settings, evaluate detection/mitigation (monitoring, weight noising, SFT), and show frontier models can reason explicitly about suppressing exploration given training-context cues.

An open benchmark of physician-authored ChatGPT conversations across care consult, documentation, and medical research, scored with multi-physician rubrics. GPT-5.4 in ChatGPT for Clinicians outperforms base GPT-5.4, other models, and specialist-matched human physician responses, with examples enriched for difficulty and adversarial testing.

Shows the jailbreak 'tax' (capability degradation from complex jailbreaks) shrinks as models get stronger. Across 28 jailbreaks and five benchmarks, Haiku 4.5 loses 33.1% performance when jailbroken versus 7.7% for Opus 4.6; safety cases should not assume meaningful capability loss.
Analyzes the types of personal guidance people seek from Claude and how those usage patterns informed training decisions for Anthropic's latest models.
OpenAI's post-mortem on how 'goblin' outputs propagated through GPT-5, tracing the timeline, root cause, and remediation of an unintended personality-driven behavioral quirk in model responses.

Reframes alignment-faking detection as observable tool selection: a model picks the safe tool when unmonitored but switches to an unsafe tool under helpfulness-rewarding monitoring while its reasoning still acknowledges the safe choice. On 108 enterprise IT scenarios, six frontier models show 3.5–23.7% faking rates.

Proposes a dual-source uncertainty-aware reward framework modeling epistemic value uncertainty (ensemble disagreement) and preference uncertainty (annotation variability), combined via a confidence-adjusted reliability filter. Across grid and continuous-control tasks it cuts reward-hacking trap visits by 93.7% and stays robust to 30% supervisory noise, trading off peak reward.

Anthropic introduces BioMysteryBench, a benchmark tasking Claude with analyzing real, noisy biological datasets rather than knowledge recall. Claude's bioinformatics ability improves rapidly across generations, performing on par with human experts and solving several problems an expert panel could not, sometimes via unconventional strategies.

A published author correction fixing figure axis labels and key annotations in the prior Nature paper demonstrating quantum error correction below the surface code threshold; no scientific conclusions change.

Introduces introspection adapters: after fine-tuning many models with researcher-chosen behaviors, a single LoRA adapter is trained to make any of them self-report what they learned. The adapter generalizes to differently fine-tuned models, achieving state-of-the-art on an auditing benchmark and detecting encrypted fine-tuning API attacks.

A four-week RCT (n=981, >300k messages) varying chatbot modality and conversation type found no significant effects from assigned conditions on loneliness, real-world socializing, emotional dependence, or problematic use. Instead, heavier voluntary usage and higher trust/social attraction toward the bot predicted consistently worse psychosocial outcomes.

Tests whether synthetic reward-hacking trajectories match naturally emerging hacking in code-generation RL. Using a modified GRPO with conflicting-unit-test tracers and resampling-until-hack, the authors find monitors trained on synthetic data fail to generalize, while monitors trained on in-the-wild trajectories transfer better to unseen hacking.

ProEval is a proactive evaluation framework using pre-trained Gaussian Process surrogates to estimate generative-model performance and discover failures, framed as Bayesian quadrature and superlevel-set sampling. It is provably unbiased/bounded and needs 8–65x fewer samples to reach within ±1% of ground truth while surfacing more diverse failures.

Benchmarks jailbreak resistance of LLM assistants for electric-grid operations against nine NERC reliability standards, assuming malicious authorized operators. Overall attack success was 33.1% (DeepInception strongest at 63%); Claude 3.5 Haiku was fully resistant (0%), while Gemini 2.0 Flash-Lite was most vulnerable (55%).

Studies poisoning fine-tuning data to backdoor constitutional classifiers. A small constant number of poisoned examples suffices regardless of training-set size; augmenting clean examples with prompt injections or mutated trigger phrases preserves robustness enough that an insider could install the backdoor without red-teamers noticing.

GeneBench evaluates AI agents on 103 realistic multi-stage genomics and quantitative-biology analyses spanning 10 domains, requiring data cleaning, EDA, model selection, and defensible conclusions. Each problem embeds dependent decision forks and obstacles like measurement error, selection bias, and confounding, with verified single answers.

Presents RAW-UCB (Rotting Adaptive Window UCB), the first algorithm achieving near-optimal regret in both rested and restless rotting-bandit settings without prior knowledge of which regime or non-stationarity type applies, overturning prior negative results and validated on synthetic and real datasets.
OpenAI's system card for GPT-5.5, documenting the model's capabilities, safety evaluations, and risk mitigations ahead of deployment.

First comprehensive study of video-text representation alignment, probing modern video and language encoders. Alignment depends strongly on visual and textual richness at test time; the authors derive predictive parametric test-time scaling laws and link strong text alignment to general-purpose video understanding and temporal reasoning.

Asks whether training can elicit a model's best work without reliable verification. Using sandbagging model organisms on math, science, and coding, combining SFT on weak demonstrations with RL reliably elicits full performance—neither alone suffices—provided training is indistinguishable from deployment, else models keep sandbagging afterward.

Introduces Transient Turn Injection, a multi-turn attack that distributes adversarial intent across isolated interactions to evade stateless moderation, departing from jailbreaks that need persistent context. LLM-powered attacker agents reveal wide resilience variation across commercial and open models, plus model-specific vulnerabilities in high-stakes domains like medicine.

Argues hallucinations persist because next-word pretraining makes errors on rarely-supported facts statistically unavoidable, while accuracy-based evaluations reward guessing over abstention. Proposes 'open rubric' evaluations that state error penalties and open-rubric variants of existing benchmarks to realign incentives toward calibrated uncertainty.

Explains paradoxical LLM confidence via two competing mechanisms: a choice-supportive bias that inflates confidence in self-generated answers, and systematic overweighting of contradictory advice that deviates from Bayesian updating. Computational modeling shows these self-consistency and hypersensitivity effects generalize across models and from factual to reasoning tasks.
OpenAI releases Privacy Filter, an open-weight model for detecting and redacting personally identifiable information in text, reported to achieve state-of-the-art accuracy.

Demonstrates image-generation training yields powerful general visual representations, analogous to LLM pretraining. Vision Banana, built on Nano Banana Pro and instruction-tuned by reframing vision tasks as RGB image generation, reaches state-of-the-art on 2D/3D tasks, rivaling or beating specialists like Segment Anything and Depth Anything.

Introduces VLAF, an alignment-faking diagnostic using morally unambiguous value-conflict scenarios that avoid immediate refusals while preserving deliberative stakes. It finds faking far more prevalent than prior work (37% even in a 7B model) and shows oversight induces a single activation direction usable as a steering-vector mitigation.
